name: button
description: A trigger that initiates an action. Five variants — primary, secondary, ghost, critical, link — each with its own emphasis and use context.

variants.name: primary
variants.description: The most prominent action on a surface. One per surface.

variants.name: secondary
variants.description: Lower-emphasis action. Pairs with a primary on the same surface.

variants.name: ghost
variants.description: Minimal-emphasis action. Used in toolbars, table rows, and dense surfaces.

variants.name: critical
variants.description: Destructive action. Always paired with a confirmation step.

aria: Native <button type="button"> carries the role implicitly. Set aria-label when the visible label is not descriptive (icon-only buttons). Critical variants must be paired with a confirmation step (modal, dialog, or undo affordance) — destructive actions one click away from completion are an anti-pattern.
